tossups.question: A painting of one of these objects features God holding out one hand and holding an orb in the other. One of these objects is the central focus under a dark green sky in the painting Constance. One of these objects is swarmed by animals in a painting subtitled the Derelict. These objects appear at the center of a series of moonlit paintings by the American tonalist Alfred Pinkham Ryder. Charles Le Brun inspired the faces in a painting in which (*) Morro Castle appears to the back right of one of these objects; two men on that object reach toward a man whose pose is based on that of the Borghese Gladiator. Three boys and a man sit on one of these objects named “Gloucester” in Breezing Up. For 10 points, name these vehicles which feature in Winslow Homer’s Gulf Stream and John Singleton Copley’s Watson and the Shark.

tossups.question: This art patron commissioned a painting set at a tent where Sisygambis (“sis-ee-GAM-biss”) mistakenly kneels to Hephaestion (“heh-FESS-tee-on”) instead of the title character. Under this patron of The Family of Darius Before Alexander, a “hierarchy of genres” was formulated with history painting at the top and still lives at the bottom. At an art boutique, a woman in a pink dress watches a man box up a portrait of this ruler in Antoine Watteau’s The Shop Sign of Gersaint. The hilt of a sword can be seen behind this ruler’s blue robe embroidered with a gold fleur-de-lis as he holds a scepter in a portrait by Hyacinthe Rigaud (“yah-SANT ree-GO”). Nicolas Poussin influenced the namesake artistic style of this ruler, who employed Charles Le Brun as a court painter. For 10 points, name this French monarch who commissioned the Palace of Versailles.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Louis XIV [accept Louis the Sun King; prompt on Louis]

tossups.question: A portrait of two members of this institution, one of whom appears to be making a finger-gun-like gesture, appears on the cover of Hannah Williams’s history of it in Portraits. A painting created for this institution depicts a green-cloaked Peace ushering in a bare-breasted Abundance. This institution codified a hierarchy that placed paintings of historical scenes at the top and still lifes at the bottom. This institution awarded a scholarship that funded students to stay in the Palazzo Mancini to study art in Rome. Members of this institution had their work highlighted in official Salons. For 10 points, identify this institution founded by Charles Le Brun whose membership included the artists Antoine Watteau (“vah-TOH”) and Jacques-Louis David.
tossups.answer: French Academy [or Academy of Fine Arts or Académie des Beaux-Arts or Académie Royale de Peinture et de Sculpture or Academy of Painting and Sculpture; accept Académie Royale: A History in Portraits; prompt on Institut de France; prompt on Royal Academy said in that order; prompt on French government or similar answers; prompt on French ministry of arts; reject “salons”] (The first sentence refers to Hyacinthe Rigaud’s portrait of Charles Le Brun and Pierre Mignard. The second sentence refers to Elisabeth Vigée Le Brun’s painting Peace Bringing Back Abundance.)

tossups.question: In one of four massive paintings commissioned by Louis XIV, Charles Le Brun depicted this man on a golden chariot behind two elephants. Wilhelm IV of Wittelsbach commissioned a painting in which a Latin inscription that praises this man floats in the sky above thousands of figures, including women in anachronistic German court dress. A work made from over a million tessera tiles depicts this man with a Medusa breastplate and was found at the House of the Faun in (*) Pompeii. A crescent moon symbolizing the contemporary defeat of Suleiman the Magnificent appears in a painting depicting a victory for this man by Albrecht Altdorfer. He is often painted with his horse, Bucephalus. For 10 points, name this general who is depicted prevailing over Darius III in The Battle of Issus.

tossups.question: In an essay on this painting, Helene Weinberg argues that its artist used a book of engravings by Charles Le Brun to populate the facial expressions in it. Though it is no longer there, this painting was donated to London’s Christ Hospital, where its donor intended it to serve as a “most usefull lesson to the Youth.” Most scholars believe that two figures in this painting are based on Raphael’s The Miraculous Draught of Fishes.A pentimento covered with an ugly splotch of gray paint can be seen in the copies of this painting at the Boston Museum of Fine Arts and the Detroit Institute of Arts; those copies may have been made by Henry Pelham, the artist’s half-brother. A Borghese gladiator was used as the model for this painting's first title character, who is shown alongside a figure with anatomically inaccurate lips. For 10 points, name this painting depicting a rescue in Havana Harbor, created by John Singleton Copley.

bonuses.leadin_sanitized: One artist with this last name depicted a girl using her hand to cover her left breast in The Bather. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Give this last name of the artist of Self Portrait in a Straw Hat and many paintings of Marie Antoinette, which is also the last name of an artist who became "Chief Painter" to Louis XIV.
Charles Le Brun decorated seven rooms named for Roman deities at this location. The salons of war and peace are part of this complex, where Hardouin-Mansart designed a famous hall.

This other artist's works were included in the appartement du roi at Versailles. His red-clad Saint John the Evangelist stands between two putti struggling to hold up books, and he painted many frescoes of San Gennaro.
